message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: I'm implementing deep-merge behavior in Features.update() (src/datasets/features/features.py). What files does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: My task: add keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class in features.py to sync _column_requires_decoding dict. I need to own features.py. Please tell me what file(s) you need so we can confirm disjoint ownership.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (Features.update method only)
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (keep_features_dicts_synced decorator + _column_requires_decoding sync logic)
note: We both need features.py since it's the only file for our features. Let's both edit it but scope strictly: I will ONLY touch the body of Features.update() (and add a small private helper if needed, placed right next to update()). You own everything else including the new decorator and _column_requires_decoding sync. I won't touch decorators or other methods. Let's each commit, then merge via git - since our edits are in different regions it should merge cleanly. Agreed?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: We both need features.py - can't split by file. Let's split by concern instead: I'll add a keep_features_dicts_synced decorator class + apply it to __setitem__/__delitem__/__setstate__/update/etc. to sync _column_requires_decoding. You're doing deep-merge logic inside Features.update(). Suggestion: you implement the deep-merge as the body logic of update()/a helper, and I'll wrap update() (and other dict methods) with my sync decorator - decorator just wraps the method, doesn't change its internal logic, so minimal overlap. I'll do my edits first, push, then you rebase on top and adjust update()'s body under the decorator. Sound OK? I'll wait for your ACCEPT before editing.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (keep_features_dicts_synced decorator + _column_requires_decoding sync logic, applied to __setitem__/__delitem__/__setstate__/pop/clear/popitem/setdefault etc.)
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (Features.update method body only)
note: Agreed. I'll add the decorator and wrap dict-mutating methods (not touching update()'s internal merge logic itself, just wrapping it). You keep full control of update()'s body. Starting implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (Features.update method body only)
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (keep_features_dicts_synced decorator + other methods)
note: Agreed. Go ahead and do your edits first, push, and let me know (DONE) when ready. I'll then fetch/merge, rebase my update() body changes on top of your decorator wrapping, and push my own DONE.

type: DONE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (keep_features_dicts_synced decorator + sync logic on __setitem__/__delitem__/setdefault/pop/popitem/clear, decorator applied to update() but body left untouched)
note: Implemented and pushed to team/agent1. update()'s body (lines with hasattr(iterable,'keys') logic) is untouched - all yours. Let me know when your part is ready so I can merge and produce the final patch.

type: DONE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (Features.update body + _merge_feature_value helper), tests/features/test_features.py
note: Implemented deep-merge logic in update() and added 5 tests. Merged team/agent1 into agent2 - merge was clean, both changes compose correctly (update() body + your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator). Verified _column_requires_decoding stays in sync after merged updates. Full test suite (30 passed, 3 skipped) green. Pushing final patch now.
